---
title: 'Thinking like the Biblical Prophets'
date: '2024-11-08T05:28:00.0050000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'NoHiding.Faith'
blogger_status: 'DRAFT'
blogger_post_id: '8570878480638115906'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/NoHiding.Faith/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Thinking Biblically'
---

<div>One of the best ways to ensure you misread the biblical authors&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>This is a commentary excerpt from the CSB Study Bible:</div><div><div></div><blockquote><div><i>The authors of the New Testament read the book of Isaiah in light of the coming of Christ and realized that this prophet anticipated Messiah’s coming with remarkable clarity. For this reason they quoted or alluded to Isaiah more than any other Old Testament book.</i></div><div><i><br /></i></div><div><i>Tremper Longman III, “Isaiah,” in CSB Study Bible: Notes, ed. Edwin A. Blum and Trevin Wax (Nashville, TN: Holman Bible Publishers, 2017), 1039.</i></div></blockquote><div></div></div><br /><br /><br />

<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;">Shalom&nbsp;<span face="sans-serif" style="background-color: white; color: #202122; font-size: 14px;">שָׁלוֹם</span>: Live Long and Prosper!</span>
<br />

<div class="MsoNormal"><span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;; font-size: 18pt;">Darrell Wolfe</span>
</div>

Storyteller | Writer | Thinker | Consultant | Freelancer | Bible Nerd
<i>*Written withs some editing and research assistance from ChatGPT-4o</i>
<br /><br /><br />
